items.title: Swalwell announces plan to resign from Congress amid sexual misconduct allegations
items.standfirst: Democratic Rep. Eric Swalwell of California says he will resign after multiple women accused him of sexual misconduct. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more.
items.content: Democratic Rep. Eric Swalwell of California says he will resign after multiple women accused him of sexual misconduct. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more.

items.title: Republican, Democratic strategists on Trump feud with Pope Leo, AI photo backlash and more
items.standfirst: President Trump is standing by his comments over the weekend targeted at Pope Leo, calling him "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy." Political strategists John McCarthy and T.W. Arrighi join with analysis.
items.content: President Trump is standing by his comments over the weekend targeted at Pope Leo, calling him "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy." Political strategists John McCarthy and T.W. Arrighi join with analysis.

items.title: Donald Trump deletes AI image of himself as Jesus after attacking Pope Leo
items.standfirst: Donald Trump launched an extraordinary attack on Pope Leo late Sunday, also going as far as to share an AI-generated image depicting himself as Jesus Christ healing the sick. He later deleted the image Monday following backlash from his usually loyal Christian and Catholic supporters, saying he “thought it was me as a doctor.”
items.content: Donald Trump launched an extraordinary attack on Pope Leo late Sunday, also going as far as to share an AI-generated image depicting himself as Jesus Christ healing the sick. He later deleted the image Monday following backlash from his usually loyal Christian and Catholic supporters, saying he “thought it was me as a doctor.”

items.title: Trump says he won't apologize to Pope Leo after criticism
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump refused to apologize to Pope Leo XIV on Monday after criticizing the pontiff for his opposition to the war in Iran — and he sought to explain away a now-deleted social media post depicting himself as Jesus by saying he had thought the image was of him as a doctor. Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has called Trump's remarks "unacceptable". FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports from Rome.
items.content: President Donald Trump refused to apologize to Pope Leo XIV on Monday after criticizing the pontiff for his opposition to the war in Iran — and he sought to explain away a now-deleted social media post depicting himself as Jesus by saying he had thought the image was of him as a doctor. Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has called Trump's remarks "unacceptable". FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports from Rome.

items.standfirst: Magyar ended Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's 16-year grip on power in a landslide victory on Sunday. The former Orbán loyalist burst onto the scene as an opposition leader in 2024.

items.title: Péter Magyar vows to pursue those who ‘plundered’ Hungary after election win
items.standfirst: Prime minister-elect promises ‘new era’ for country after defeating far-right Viktor OrbánHungary’s prime minister-elect, Péter Magyar, has pledged to pursue those who “plundered, looted, betrayed, indebted and ruined” his country, promising “a new era” after a landslide election victory over his far-right predecessor Viktor Orbán.Magyar, whose centre-right Tisza party won at least 138 of the 199 seats in parliament, said the full election results should be confirmed by 4 May and he hoped his government could be installed the next day. Continue reading...

items.title: Electoral earthquake: Hungary vote triggers setback for Putin and Trump
items.standfirst: Hungarian voters on Sunday ousted long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, rejecting the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement that he embodied in favor of a pro-European challenger in a bombshell election result with global repercussions. It was a stunning blow for Orbán — a close ally of both US President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin — who conceded defeat after what he called a ″painful″ election result.
items.content: Hungarian voters on Sunday ousted long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, rejecting the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement that he embodied in favor of a pro-European challenger in a bombshell election result with global repercussions. It was a stunning blow for Orbán — a close ally of both US President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin — who conceded defeat after what he called a ″painful″ election result.

items.title: Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary
items.standfirst: After 16 years in power, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat in the country's parliamentary election as Peter Magyar’s center-right Tisza Party was projected to win. NBC News' Molly Hunter reports on the landslide result that could affect the European Union and Ukraine.
items.content: After 16 years in power, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat in the country's parliamentary election as Peter Magyar’s center-right Tisza Party was projected to win. NBC News' Molly Hunter reports on the landslide result that could affect the European Union and Ukraine.

items.title: Trump says US will blockade strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks fail
items.standfirst: Iran warns move would breach ceasefire as US president also repeats threats to strike critical infrastructureMiddle East crisis – live updatesDonald Trump has said the US will begin blockading the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to take control of the strategic waterway from Iran in the aftermath of failed peace negotiations between the countries in Pakistan.The US president also threatened to bomb Iran’s water treatment facilities, power plants and bridges if Tehran did not agree to abandon its nuclear weapons programme, the key sticking point between the two sides. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Collapse of US-Iran talks heightens fears of prolonged energy shock
items.standfirst: Oil prices and borrowing costs are expected to rise this week as tankers remain stranded in the GulfMiddle East crisis live – latest updatesThe failure of the US and Iran to reach a peace deal after marathon negotiations has put markets on alert for further oil and gas price rises.With large numbers of oil tankers remaining stuck in the Gulf, the US vice-president, JD Vance, blamed the collapse of the talks on Tehran’s refusal to abandon its nuclear weapons programme, while Iranian sources hit back at “excessive” demands from Washington. Continue reading...

items.title: Texas Man Charged With Attempted Murder in Altman Attack
items.standfirst: Local and federal prosecutors filed attempted murder and other criminal charges against the man arrested Friday for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at the home of OpenAI Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman.
items.content: Local and federal prosecutors filed attempted murder and other criminal charges against the man arrested Friday for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at the home of OpenAI Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman.

items.title: FBI searches home of suspect in Molotov cocktail attack on Sam Altman's house
items.standfirst: FBI agents are searching the home of a man suspected of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home. CBS News chief correspondent Matt Gutman has the latest.
items.content: FBI agents are searching the home of a man suspected of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home. CBS News chief correspondent Matt Gutman has the latest.

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Texas Rep. Tony Gonzales to step down as he faces expulsion vote
items.standfirst: The Texas Republican faces an expulsion vote stemming from his affair with a staffer who later died by suicide.
items.content: The Texas Republican faces an expulsion vote stemming from his affair with a staffer who later died by suicide.

items.title: Republican Tony Gonzales to step down from Congress amid expulsion threat
items.standfirst: Gonzales, who admitted affair with aide who later died by suicide, says he will file retirement from office on TuesdayRepresentative Tony Gonzales, a Republican from Texas, announced on Monday he was stepping down from Congress after acknowledging an extramarital affair with a staffer.Gonzales, who was facing a growing threat of expulsion by his colleagues, admitted last month that he had an affair with an aide who later died by suicide. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Starmer’s ‘corrosive complacency’ on defence has put UK in peril, says ex-Nato chief
clusters.neutral_headline: Starmer accused of ‘corrosive complacency’ on UK defence by former Nato chief
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Financial Times

items.title: Starmer’s ‘corrosive complacency’ on defence has put UK in peril, says ex-Nato chief
items.standfirst: George Robertson says Iran war should be wake-up call to address military underfunding in scathing remarksThe British government has shown a “corrosive complacency towards defence” and put the UK “in peril”, according to a government adviser, in fierce criticisms of Keir Starmer’s military policy.The former Nato secretary general and author of the government’s strategic defence review, George Robertson, believes Starmer was “not willing to make the necessary investment”, the Financial Times has reported. Continue reading...
items.content: George Robertson says Iran war should be wake-up call to address military underfunding in scathing remarksThe British government has shown a “corrosive complacency towards defence” and put the UK “in peril”, according to a government adviser, in fierce criticisms of Keir Starmer’s military policy.The former Nato secretary general and author of the government’s strategic defence review, George Robertson, believes Starmer was “not willing to make the necessary investment”, the Financial Times has reported. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez’s wife charged with corruption after years-long probe
clusters.neutral_headline: Spanish PM's wife charged with corruption after two-year probe
bullet_summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez’s wife, Begona Gomez, has been formally charged with corruption following a years-long investigation, a court ruling said on Monday
Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez’s wife charged with corruption after years-long probe
items.standfirst: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez’s wife, Begona Gomez, has been formally charged with corruption following a years-long investigation, a court ruling said on Monday. The probe, opened in 2024, examines whether she used her position for private gain, allegations she and Sanchez deny, increasing pressure on his minority government.
items.content: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez’s wife, Begona Gomez, has been formally charged with corruption following a years-long investigation, a court ruling said on Monday. The probe, opened in 2024, examines whether she used her position for private gain, allegations she and Sanchez deny, increasing pressure on his minority government.

items.title: Spanish PM's wife charged with corruption after two-year probe
items.standfirst: Begoña Gómez is accused of using her relationship with Pedro Sánchez to advance her private career.
items.content: Begoña Gómez is accused of using her relationship with Pedro Sánchez to advance her private career.

items.content: Officials warn cyanide could harm marine life and weaken the reef supporting a grounded warship.

items.title: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
items.standfirst: Beijing rejects the allegation as a "farce", while Manila claims the poison was used to kill fish and deprive troops stationed in the area of food.
items.content: Beijing rejects the allegation as a "farce", while Manila claims the poison was used to kill fish and deprive troops stationed in the area of food.

clusters.title: Peru election extended for second day after ballot delivery failure
clusters.neutral_headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
bullet_summary: Peru police on Monday said they arrested an election official amid an enquiry into the voting delays
Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: Peru election extended for second day after ballot delivery failure
items.standfirst: Peruvians headed back to the polls Monday after ballot shortages forced an unprecedented extension of Sunday’s election, reflecting deep strains in a country crippled by political instability and escalating crime. Peru police on Monday said they arrested an election official amid an enquiry into the voting delays.
items.content: Peruvians headed back to the polls Monday after ballot shortages forced an unprecedented extension of Sunday’s election, reflecting deep strains in a country crippled by political instability and escalating crime. Peru police on Monday said they arrested an election official amid an enquiry into the voting delays.

items.title: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
items.standfirst: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.
items.content: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

items.title: The Internet's Most Powerful Archiving Tool Is in Peril
items.standfirst: As major news outlets cut off the Wayback Machine, journalists and advocacy groups are rallying to protect the Internet Archive’s vast collection of web pages.
